stories.title: Estonia unmasks record number of Russian spies
stories.summary: Estonia apprehended a record number of Russian collaborators in 2025, the country’s Internal Security Service (KAPO) revealed on Monday. At least nine individuals were identified as “agents” in the intelligence service’s latest annual report , and the residency permits of several clergy members with ties to the Russian Orthodox Church were revoked on security grounds. The total number of people detected to be working for the Kremlin, or detained or expelled for promoting Russia

stories.title: Zelenskyy will fix Druzhba oil pipeline as he counts on Hungary to lift EU loan veto
stories.summary: BERLIN — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Tuesday that the damaged Druzhba oil pipeline would be operational by the end of April, but there’s a trade-off. Zelenskyy expects Hungary to lift its veto on the €90 billion loan to fund Kyiv’s war effort against Russia, as he confirmed oil will flow through the conduit again by end of April. “We have promised that the [Druzhba pipeline] will be repaired by the end of April. Not completely, but enough for it to be operational,&#8

stories.title: Viktor Orbán inspired rightwingers across the EU and in Britain. His defeat could represent a turning of the tide | Polly Toynbee
stories.summary: We must hope this vote will be the start of a wider backlash – and send hard-right populism back to the fringes where it belongs The forces of darkness rolled back on Sunday. The mighty combined power of Vladimir Putin’s Russia and Donald Trump’s America were defeated in Hungary, as European liberal democratic values triumphed . The populist-nativist right put their all into keeping Viktor Orbán in power. The US vice-president, JD Vance, mid-war in Iran, took time out to parade his patronage in

stories.title: Zelenskyy hails Magyar’s win over Orbán as ‘the victory of light over darkness’ in Hungary – Europe live
stories.summary: Ukrainian leader hopes for ‘pragmatic’ and ‘friendly’ relations with new government in contrast with hostility of previous pro-Russian regime in Berlin At his press conference with Zelenskyy, Germany’s chancellor Friedrich Merz also welcomed Péter Magyar’s decisive victory, saying it would have “implications for our support for Ukraine”. “More Hungarians than ever before cast their votes. By an overwhelming majority, they voted not only to oust a government, but to oust an entire system.” “Volod

stories.title: EU relieved to see back of Orbán but Magyar’s Hungary may still present problems
stories.summary: Even if migration policy and lukewarm support for Ukraine will raise tensions, Hungary will now be arguing for its own interests rather than Russia’s What Magyar’s victory means for EU and Hungary In Brussels, the relief was palpable after the defeat of Viktor Orbán, the EU leader who recently declared himself at Vladimir Putin’s service . For the EU, Péter Magyar’s victory was all the sweeter, as voters decisively rejected Orbán’s fear-mongering campaign that sought to portray him in cahoots wi

stories.title: ‘We were never friends’: Kremlin plays down loss of ally following Orbán’s election defeat
stories.summary: Loss of closest European ally will force Kremlin to consider whether non-autocratic states can ever be reliable partners Europe live – latest updates The Kremlin said on Tuesday it was pleased that Hungary’s prime minister-elect, Péter Magyar, appeared open to pragmatic dialogue, as Moscow adopts a wait-and-see approach after the election loss of its closest partner in Europe, Viktor Orbán. “For now, we can note with satisfaction, as far as we understand, his [Magyar’s] willingness to engage in

stories.title: Will Orbán’s defeat in Hungary be a turning point for Europe? – The Latest
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule has come to an end after the opposition Tisza party, led by Péter Magyar, won the Hungary election by a landslide. Orbán quickly conceded after what he described as a 'painful but unambiguous' result. His defeat will come as a blow to Donald Trump, who has repeatedly endorsed Orbán and whose vice-president, JD Vance, had travelled to Budapest days before the election. All eyes are now on Magyar to see how Hungary’s relationship with the EU and Nato could change after

stories.title: The Guardian view on Hungary’s election: democracy reclaimed | Editorial
stories.summary: The defeat of Orbánism is a globally significant political moment. But it is, above all, a triumph for the citizens who mobilised to take their country back Prior to his landslide election victory in 2010, which was to lead to 16 unbroken years in power, Viktor Orbán would tell supporters: “We have only to win once, but then properly.” Achieving a so-called supermajority by winning two-thirds of parliamentary seats allowed Mr Orbán to change the constitution, and begin turning Hungary into

stories.title: Orban's defeat in Hungary reverberates across Central Europe
stories.summary: Viktor Orban's crushing defeat in the April 12 election will have far-reaching consequences for Europe's political landscape — and especially for neighboring Czechia and Slovakia, which are run by his political allies.

stories.title: After Orbán loss, Europe’s far right reassesses MAGA endorsement
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán’s defeat in the Hungarian elections suggests that political endorsements from Donald Trump or JD Vance do not necessarily boost European far-right campaigns, as the MAGA message increasingly struggles to resonate in Europe.

stories.title: 'We cannot ask any country to give up its territory,' Magyar says on Ukraine
stories.summary: Outgoing PM Viktor Orbán had largely centred his electoral campaign on Ukraine and Volodymyr Zelenskyy, blaming Kyiv for the Druzhba pipeline blockade to accusations of meddling in Hungarian elections. Will things change with Magyar in power?

stories.title: Vance’s bad week: vice-president risks becoming face of two Trump foreign policy failures
stories.summary: Orbán is out in Hungary and talks have failed to end the war in Iran – ill-fated road trip has been setback for Maga aims Shortly before JD Vance’s ill-fated week crisscrossing the world, Donald Trump asked him during a private Easter brunch about how the Iran negotiations were shaping up. “If it doesn’t happen, I’m blaming JD Vance,” Trump said to laughs in the room. “If it does happen, I’m taking full credit.” The joke at Vance’s expense contained an unfortunate nugget of truth: this is not an

stories.title: ‘Now I’m hopeful’: Hungarians welcome the morning after 16 years of Orbán rule
stories.summary: Excitement and disbelief mix on the streets of Budapest after record turnout drives opposition’s landslide victory Péter Magyar vows to pursue those who ‘plundered’ Hungary, after election win In a small plaza facing Budapest’s Nyugati train station, Gabor, 40, confessed that he was very, very hungover. “I partied all night,” he said, laughing. “It was crazy, I thought Fidesz was going to win. Now I’m really hopeful.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Hungary: Orban's election defeat sparks hope and relief
stories.summary: Viktor Orban's Fidesz party was swept aside in Hungary's parliamentary election. His defeat is a blow for Putin, Trump and populists the world over. Election winner Peter Magyar is promising major changes.

stories.title: EU applauds Hungary’s election result
stories.summary: After Hungary’s opposition ousted Viktor Orban after 16 years in power, hopes rise in Brussels that Budapest may abandon its obstructionist course. What is the EU expecting from Peter Magyar?

stories.title: Hungarian voters celebrate 'much-needed' change
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by 45-year-old ex-party insider Péter Magyar.

stories.title: Hungary's next PM would pick up if Putin calls and tell him to stop Ukraine war
stories.summary: Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations, but Péter Magyar says he will not call Vladimir Putin himself.

stories.title: A Petőfi TV műsorvezetője április 13-án: Szeretnénk nyitni azok felé az alkotók felé, akik eddig még nem szerepeltek nálunk
stories.summary: Magyar Péter szerdán a közmédia vendége lesz. Több település polgármestere közölte, hogy nem fizetik be a szolidaritási hozzájárulást, megvárják az új kormány megalakulását.

stories.title: Tisza has won. What happens next in Hungary until the new government is formed?
stories.summary: The President must convene the inaugural session of the new parliament by May 12, at which time he will propose a candidate for prime minister. Until then, the government’s scope of action remains unrestricted, and the Fidesz-controlled parliament can still pass any legislation. However, a two-thirds Tisza majority can subsequently repeal these laws.

stories.title: Agresszív technikák tették a világ legkelendőbb terápiájává a forradalmi rákgyógyszert
stories.summary: A forradalmi immunterápiás rákgyógyszer, a Keytruda néhány év alatt a világ legkelendőbb gyógyszere lett, miközben sok országban a betegek számára szinte megfizethetetlen. Egy nemzetközi újságírócsapat, amelynek a Direkt36 is tagja volt, egyéves munkával tárta fel, milyen módszereket alkalmaz a szer amerikai gyártója, hogy magasan tartsa az árát, miközben igyekszik késleltetni az olcsóbb alternatívák piacra jutását.

stories.title: 15 millió forint értékben lopott ezüstöt és imalepleket egy budapesti zsinagógából, az egyiket egy piacon passzolták el
stories.summary: Az 55 éves, többszörösen büntetett előéletű férfit letartóztatták.

stories.title: A kutyapártos Nagy Dávid szerint rosszul mérték fel, hogy az embereknek többre van igényük, mint hogy ne Orbán legyen a miniszterelnök
stories.summary: A Kutyapárt vasárnap lemondott pártigazgatója szerint bebizonyosodott, hogy hazugság, miszerint megélhetési politikusok lennének.

stories.title: Újabb magyar kosarast draftoltak a világ legerősebb női ligájába
stories.summary: A 19 éves Rátkai Esztert a Phoenix Mercury csapata választotta ki.

stories.title: Forint, kötvény, részvény: ezért fogadta a Tisza győzelmét hurráoptimizmussal a tőkepiac
stories.summary: Annyit erősödött a forint, hogy hirtelen három uniós országot is lehagytunk az átlagbérekben, sőt valójában inflációval korrigálva még soha nem volt ilyen drága az euróval szemben a magyar deviza. Igazán nagy lökést egy hiteles euróövezeti csatlakozási menetrend adhatna még.

stories.title: Az RMDSZ torz valóságra épített évtizedes politikájának áldozata lett az erdélyi magyar közösség
stories.summary: Székely István Gergő politológus szerint az RMDSZ átvette és felerősítette Erdélyben a fideszes politikai narratívát, ami rövid távon hatékony mobilizációs eszköz volt, viszont az erdélyi magyar közösséget politikai zsákutcába vezette.

stories.title: Folytatást kapott a Magyar Nemzet Radnai Márk-os cikkek miatti helyreigazítás-sorozata
stories.summary: Újabb négy helyreigazítás jelent meg a Magyar Nemzet címlapján.

stories.title: A polgármester szerint méltatlanná vált hivatalára a Magyar Pétert trágár stílusban gyalázó piliscsabai jegyző
stories.summary: Ezért megtette a szükséges jogi lépéseket a köztisztviselői törvény alapján.

stories.title: The leaked Szijjártó wiretaps indicate that neither he nor Orbán understood that Europe is not a game
stories.summary: Once they saw that behind the scenes warnings fell on deaf ears, foreign powers turned to other means of getting their message through to Orbán. What the prime minister clearly didn’t grasp was that when you're playing with the big boys, but actively help the opposing team, sooner or later your teammates will have had enough.

stories.title: A Kreml elégedetten nyugtázta, hogy tudomásuk szerint Magyar hajlandó pragmatikus párbeszédre Oroszországgal
stories.summary: Ha így van, a szándék és akarat kölcsönös, a továbbiak majd kiderülnek az új magyar kormány lépéseiből – fogalmazott Dmitrij Peszkov, Putyin sajtótitkára.

stories.title: Recseg-ropog a védett üzemanyagár rendszere, és éppen a politikai átmenet időszakában kellene hozzányúlni
stories.summary: Jó sok pénzbe kerül, hogy Magyarország még valahogy tartja magát kőolajkészletben és üzemanyagokban, például az USA-tól vettünk még a mostani szinthez képest is túlárazottnak tűnő olajat. De a tartalékok így is nagyon fogynak.

stories.title: A román miniszterelnök tanácsadója arra kéri Magyar Pétert, vizsgálják ki a Romániába juttatott közpénzek sorsát is
stories.summary: A korábbi EP-képviselő levelet írt Magyar Péternek.

stories.title: Egy új, pozitív fejezetben bíznak a nemzetközi befektetők, akik elkezdték felmérni az Orbán utáni országot
stories.summary: Sokat várnak az EU-s kapcsolatok javulásától, illetve az ennek következményeként felszabaduló források hatásától.

stories.title: Franciaország egyik legrangosabb múzeumában nyílt önálló kiállítása Ferenczy Károlynak
stories.summary: A magyar modernizmus kiemelkedő alkotója először mutatkozik be nagyszabású, monografikus kiállítás keretében külföldön.

stories.title: Az utolsó héten még neki kampányolt, mégse lepődött meg J. D. Vance Orbán vereségén
stories.summary: Az amerikai alelnök szerint nem azért látogatott Budapestre, mert biztosak voltak Orbán győzelmében.

stories.title: A Roszatom vezetője Magyarról: Ismeri a paksi projektet, hiszen Orbán kormányában dolgozott
stories.summary: Alekszej Lihacsov fenntartja mérsékelt optimizmusát a paksi bővítés folytatásával kapcsolatban, egy olyan életrajzi elemet említve a Tisza párt vezetőjéről, amely valójában nem igaz, hiszen Magyar Péternek nem volt kormányzati pozíciója.

stories.title: Von der Leyen és Magyar Péter újra tárgyaltak, gyorsan kell dolgozni a jogállamiság helyreállításán
stories.summary: Ursula von der Leyen ismét egyeztetett Magyar Péterrel.

stories.title: Orbán Balázs beszorult a visszhangkamrába – élőben a választás után kifakadó Ferencz Orsolya űrbiztossal
stories.summary: Kiket kellene kivágni végre a nemzeti oldal testéből a Fidesz veresége után? A Telexen beszél először részletesen a miniszteri biztos a választás másnapján nagyot futó videójáról.

stories.title: Hadházy a Telexnek: Nagyon könnyű lenne lecsukni Orbánt, Lázárt és Szijjártót, csak szándék kérdése
stories.summary: A leköszönő képviselő a Telex élő műsorában arról is beszélt, hogy hogy élte meg a Tisza győzelmét és folytatja-e a korrupcióellenes munkáját.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a közmédiában: reggel fél nyolckor a Kossuth rádióban kezd, aztán az M1 vendége lesz
stories.summary: „A szemünk előtt hullik darabjaira a pártállam” -írta a meghívásról posztjában a Tisza párt elnöke, akit másfél éve láthattuk utoljára a közmédiában.

stories.title: What next for Hungary as country celebrates Péter Magyar victory over Viktor Orbán?
stories.summary: People in the capital, Budapest, and across Hungary celebrated the victory of the opposition party Tisza, led by Péter Magyar, which has won the parliamentary elections, putting an end to the 16-year rule of the populist Viktor Orbán. The Guardian's Europe reporter Jakub Krupa reports from the Hungarian capital Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power Europe live – latest updates Continue reading...

stories.title: Hungary election: Supporters of Peter Magyar's party celebrate in Budapest
stories.summary: Supporters of Peter Magyar, leader of the pro-European conservative TISZA party, celebrate on the banks on the river Danube in Budapest.

stories.title: Poland and South Korea upgrade ties to strategic defence partnership deal
stories.summary: During a visit to Seoul on Monday, Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk and South Korean President Lee Jae-myung pledged to strengthen their cooperation, particularly in defence.

stories.title: Thousands celebrate in Hungary as Péter Magyar ends Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule – video
stories.summary: Thousands flooded the streets as Hungary's opposition leader, Péter Magyar, declared victory after his Tisza party claimed a landslide win over Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party. The victory ends Orbán's 16-year rule and marks a significant shift for Hungary's relationship with the EU and Russia. Orbán was celebrated by conservatives across Europe and the US, but lost favour at home with voters who grew weary of economic stagnation, international isolation and oligarchs amassing wealth. His landslide

stories.title: Viktor Orbán is gone. What does his fall mean for Europe? Our panel responds
stories.summary: Hungary’s return to democracy will be hard. But the impact of Péter Magyar’s decisive victory could be profound, inside the country and beyond Continue reading...

stories.title: Hungary: Peter Magyar's election heralds thaw in EU ties
stories.summary: Following the ouster of Russia-friendly Viktor Orban after 16 years, Europe is now waiting to see if his designated successor Peter Magyar can make good on his promises to repair ties with Brussels.

stories.title: Viktor Orbán's Hungarian experiment runs out of steam
stories.summary: Péter Magyar's historic win offers peace for a country exhausted by the tensions of Viktor Orbán's rule, Nick Thorpe reports from Budapest.

stories.title: Hungary: jubilant scenes across Budapest follow Magyar’s victory
stories.summary: Hungary saw large crowds take to the streets on Sunday, 12 April 2026, after Prime Minister Viktor Orbán conceded defeat, ending 16 years in power.

stories.title: Katya Adler: Jubilation in Budapest will be felt in Europe but leaves Moscow cold
stories.summary: European leaders share Hungarians' joy over the ousting of Vladimir Putin's EU ally, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
stories.summary: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

stories.title: Hungary election: Orban concedes 'painful' defeat to Magyar
stories.summary: Veteran Prime Minister Viktor Orban has acknowledged the landslide victory of Peter Magyar and his Tisza party in Hungary's parliamentary election. Magyar has promised sweeping reforms.

stories.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
stories.summary: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.

stories.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough Ukraine stance, poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

stories.title: As Hungary gears up for a historic election, DW answers a few key questions
stories.summary: Hungary's election on April 12 is the most important for the state since 1989, and also of historic significance for the EU. DW takes a more detailed look.

stories.title: Hungary: Scandal, fear and hope ahead of vote
stories.summary: Fake news and a growing number of political scandals: Hungary hasn't seen an election this fraught since after 1989's collapse of the communist dictatorship.

stories.title: Italy’s Meloni halts defense pact with Israel
stories.summary: ROME — Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has suspended Italy’s defense cooperation agreement with Israel as she continues to distance her government from the U.S.-Israeli war in Iran that has enveloped the Middle East. Speaking on the fringes of a wine industry event in the northern city of Verona on Tuesday, Meloni told reporters her government “has decided to suspend the automatic renewal of the defense agreement with Israel in consideration of the current situation.” The 2003 de

stories.title: Angus Taylor accused of ‘desperate dog-whistle’ immigration speech as Pauline Hanson takes credit
stories.summary: Tony Burke says millions of people will be asking why the Liberals have a problem with their parents ‘who don’t speak great English but are great Australians’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor has accused Angus Taylor of “desperate dog-whistling” and says millions of Australians will be asking why the Liberal party has a problem with their parents who don’t speak English, amid a backlash to the opposition’s n

stories.title: Detention of journalist in Kuwait raises questions about crackdown on freedom of speech
stories.summary: Ahmed Shihab-Eldin was arrested after reporting on friendly fire incident during US conflict with Iran Middle East crisis live – latest updates The detention of a prize-winning international journalist over his reporting of a friendly fire incident in Kuwait is raising questions about the crackdown on freedom of speech across the Middle East as a result of the US-Israel war with Iran, the Committee to Protect Journalists has warned. Ahmed Shihab-Eldin, born in the US and a Kuwaiti national, was

stories.title: Survivors ask why busy market bombed in Nigerian anti-terror campaign
stories.summary: Military has described devastating attack that killed up to 200 people, many of them civilians, as a ‘precision airstrike’ Survivors and observers have questioned the Nigerian military’s rationale for a devastating airstrike on a busy market that killed as many as 200 people, many of them civilians. The hit on Jilli market on the border of the north-eastern Borno and Yobe states on Saturday is the latest in a string of attacks by the country’s air force over the past decade with a high civilian

stories.title: First Thing: US starts naval blockade of Iranian ports after deadline passes
stories.summary: Iran says Americans will be hit with higher fuel prices due to the blockade. Plus, summers are growing longer globally Don’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up here Good morning. The US has begun blocking ships from using Iranian ports in the Gulf , transforming the US-Israeli war against Iran into a test of economic endurance. How could Centcom enforce the blockade? It is unclear, but as a missile strike on a tanker attempting to break the blockade could cause an environmental disas

stories.title: Chernobyl could face 'catastrophic' collapse as repairs stall following Russian drone strike
stories.summary: Failure to repair the protective structure around the nuclear site could unleash 'highly radioactive dust' that 'does not recognise borders', experts warn.

stories.title: Robots captured Russian army positions for first time in history, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: KYIV — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said the Ukrainian army captured a Russian position using only ground robotic systems and unmanned aerial vehicles. “For the first time in the history of this war, an enemy position was taken exclusively by unmanned platforms — ground systems and drones,” Zelenskyy said on Monday. “The occupiers surrendered, and the operation was carried out without infantry and without losses on our side,” Zelenskyy added. The Ukrainian army has been scaling up the use of gr

stories.title: France won’t fast-track plan to keep shops open on Labor Day
stories.summary: PARIS — The French government won’t rush to pass a bill that would have allowed businesses such as bakeries, flower shops and butchers to stay open on May 1. Lawmakers from the conservative Les Républicains and French President Emmanuel Macron’s centrist party last week tried to effectively fast-track the measure through the parliamentary process in the hopes of getting the legislation passed by this year’s Labor Day holiday. But Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu, who has throug

stories.title: China’s Xi recognizes Spain’s Sánchez as key interlocutor between Beijing and the EU
stories.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping on Tuesday committed to strengthening relations with the EU following a meeting with Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez in Beijing. “Let us establish a stronger bond between China and the European Union,” Xi said at the Great Hall of the People. “If these powers understand each other and cooperate, it will benefit their societies and also contribute to the stability, peace, and prosperity of the world in this delicate international climate.” Xi also recognized Sánc

stories.title: France and UK to co-host talks on securing Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: PARIS — French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will co-host a videoconference Friday to map out a plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz, their offices said. The two leaders are to lead talks on forming a “multilateral and purely defensive mission” that would help “restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz when the security conditions allow it,” the Elysée Palace said in a statement. The statement said that “non-bellig

stories.title: Mark Carney secures majority government in Canada after special election win
stories.summary: Carney’s Liberals will now be able to pass legislation without the support of opposition parties – and govern until 2029 The Canadian prime minister, Mark Carney, has secured a parliamentary majority for his Liberal government, CBC News reported. The victory will help him push through a legislative agenda he says is needed for an increasingly divided geopolitical world. Three special elections were held on Monday in Ontario and Quebec, with two in districts – known as ridings – that have long vo

stories.title: Searching for Satyrus review – on the hunt for an elusive butterfly and the lepidopterist who named them
stories.summary: Rena Effendi attempts to find the species named after her wayward, womanising father – and a connection to the man she never knew – in this moving documentary Photojournalist Rena Effendi’s father was famous in the world of butterflies; he was a lepidopterist who spent seven years hunting one species. Effendi remarks drily that seven years was longer than Rustam Effendi lasted in any of his four – possibly more – marriages. (When asked for a precise headcount of wives, his old friend answers: “G

stories.title: Taylor’s Trumpian immigration pitch is aimed at fighting One Nation – but puts Australia’s identity at stake
stories.summary: The Coalition might be in opposition to Labor but it is hoping to stem its loss of voters to Pauline Hanson Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast It was not long ago that the notion of Australia as the world’s most successful multicultural country was not just a bipartisan political position but a source of national pride, expressed as enthusiastically by Liberals as their Labor counterparts. “I believe, no, I know that Australia is the most successful multicultural immigr

stories.title: Gina Rinehart and rival heirs brace for court verdict on claim to billion-dollar fortune
stories.summary: Judgment will rule on whether spoils of some of Hancock Prospecting’s iron ore projects must be shared with family of her father’s business partner Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Gina Rinehart faces the possibility of losing billions of dollars in riches from her Pilbara iron ore empire and her mantle as Australia’s wealthiest person when a long-awaited court verdict is delivered in Perth on Wednesday. The Western Australian supreme court judgment will rule on wheth

stories.title: Spanish prime minister’s wife charged with corruption
stories.summary: Pedro Sánchez’s wife, Begoña Gómez, and two others charged after investigation triggered by group with far-right links Begoña Gómez, the wife of Spain’s prime minister, Pedro Sánchez, has been charged with embezzlement, influence peddling, corruption in business dealings and misappropriation of funds at the end of a two-year investigation by a judge in Madrid. Gómez, 55, has been accused of using her influence as the wife of the socialist prime minister to secure and manage a post at Madrid’s Co

stories.title: United Airlines CEO reportedly pitched merger with American, sparking competition fears
stories.summary: Critics warned of ‘higher ticket prices, more fees, and fewer options’ for passengers if two travel giants try to combine The CEO of United Airlines is said to have pitched a blockbuster merger with American Airlines during a meeting with Donald Trump, floating the combination of the world’s two largest carriers. Scott Kirby, who leads United, raised the prospect during an encounter with the US president in late February, Reuters reported, citing two unnamed sources. Such a deal would overhaul t

stories.title: Officials who made errors before Southport murders ‘may still be making same mistakes’
stories.summary: Former victims’ commissioner Vera Baird says disciplinary action is essential to ensure people are held accountable • UK politics live – latest updates Families in Southport cannot be sure that officials who made “catastrophic” errors before the murder of three girls are not still making the same mistakes, a former victims’ commissioner has said. Vera Baird KC said all those who failed to properly monitor the killer, Axel Rudakubana, should be held “personally accountable” and that authorities m

stories.title: EHRC updates guidance on how to apply supreme court ruling on gender
stories.summary: Changes follow concern that original code created a legal minefield for organisations implementing it The equalities watchdog has updated its guidance on how to implement the supreme court ruling on gender after the government requested changes to the original proposals submitted last year. stories.title: Reeves condemns Trump’s decision to launch war against Iran as ‘folly’– UK politics live
stories.summary: Chancellor ‘frustrated and angry’ at the effect on UK firms and families and says US went into war without a clear exit plan Q: Why are you calling for an inquiry into Nigel Farage’s investment in a bitcoin firm ? Davey said that, in investing in crypto, Farage, the Reform UK leader, seemed to be copying Donald Trump. He said he thought MPs should be banned from promoting financial services or products. [Farage is] now promoting this business. The question is, is he persuading people to put mone

stories.title: Brazil’s former spy chief who fled country arrested by ICE agents in US
stories.summary: Alexandre Ramagem fled country after he was sentenced to 16 years for his role in plotting military coup in Brazil When Brazil ’s former president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to nearly 30 years in prison for an attempted coup, six other members of his cabinet were also found guilty and all began serving their sentences – except for one. Days before the verdict, Alexandre Ramagem, Bolsonaro’s former spy chief, fled by car to Guyana and boarded a flight to the United States, where he has remained

stories.title: Iran war: Tehran slams US port blockade
stories.summary: Iran has called the US blockade of its ports a "grave violation" of its sovereignty. US President Donald Trump has said Iranian representatives have asked for a deal following fruitless peace talks. DW has more.

stories.title: Canada: Carney secures majority after special election
stories.summary: The Liberal Party's two by-election wins secured a majority in Canada's parliament, allowing them to pass legislation without opposition support.

stories.title: The world ditched wasteful toilets, the US stayed behind
stories.summary: US toilets use far more water than many of their global counterparts. President Donald Trump is pushing to loosen water pressure standards, a move critics say would increase waste.

stories.title: Germany: Man found guilty in Pelicot-style rape case
stories.summary: A Chinese student has been found guilty of drugging and raping a woman in Munich over a period of months. The perpetrator was a member of a Telegram chat group where men organized rapes and shared images of their crimes.

stories.title: Germany, Ukraine discuss drone deal as Merz hosts Zelenskyy
stories.summary: EU membership for Ukraine, European involvement in peace talks and a comprehensive ​bilateral drone deal were all on the agenda during German-Ukrainian government consultations. DW has more.

stories.title: Haiti: Several police arrested over stampede which killed 25
stories.summary: The arrests included five police officers. The deadly stampede occurred at a former military citadel in the north of the country.

stories.title: Trump says US military blockaded Iranian ports to ramp up pressure on Tehran
stories.summary: The US blockade comes in an attempt to economically pressure Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to maritime traffic after its six-week closure caused global oil prices to surge. Roughly 40 vessels have passed through since the start of the ceasefire, down from 100 or more per day before the war.

stories.title: Greek police using masked migrants to forcibly push other migrants back across border
stories.summary: Mercenaries recruited since at least 2020 for illegal migrant ‘pushbacks’ in Greece, BBC told

stories.title: Trump refuses to apologise after clash with Pope Leo XIV over Iran war
stories.summary: Despite widespread criticism after the US president lambasted the US-born pontiff as being "weak on crime," Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni's rare rebuke, Trump said no to an apology to the head of the Catholic Church while reiterating his claims.

stories.title: Sixteen injured in rare Turkey school shooting in Siverek district
stories.summary: Turkey was rocked by a school shooting on Tuesday 14 April 2026 when an 18 year old former student opened fire at Ahmet Koyuncu Vocational and Technical Anatolian High School in Siverek, in Şanlıurfa province.

stories.title: Espionage, sabotage, attacks: German intelligence agency sends warning to defence industry
stories.summary: In March, the Federal Office for the Protection of the Constitution is said to have approached the defence industry to press for stricter security standards.

stories.title: UK sets up Brexit-style crisis team to deal with Iran conflict
stories.summary: LONDON — Keir Starmer is establishing a Brexit-style “Middle East response committee” to overhaul how Britain deals with the Iran conflict. The U.K. prime minister will chair the group for the first time Tuesday in an effort to create a central structure underpinning Britain’s international and domestic response and developing policy, said a U.K. government official not authorized to speak publicly. It will comprise two groups, one ministerial and one at official level, an

stories.title: Iran tries to cosy up to Europe to increase pressure on US
stories.summary: Regime hopes to capitalise on deepening transatlantic split by briefing previously sidelined European countries In a move designed to increase pressure on the US to make compromises in its conflict with his country, Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi has been briefing European capitals on the nature of the offer Iran had been willing to make about its stockpile of highly enriched uranium and future stewardship of the strait of Hormuz during the weekend talks in Islamabad. After the inconclu

stories.title: Hanson claims credit for immigration plans; PM arrives in Brunei – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Angus Taylor accused of ‘desperate dog-whistle’ immigration speech as Pauline Hanson takes credit Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Sydney is getting its first new Roman Catholic Cathedral in more than a century, part of a massive 7.7-hectare integrated precinct in the northern suburb of Waitara. stories.title: China Evergrande’s billionaire boss pleads guilty to fraud
stories.summary: Hui Ka Yan expresses remorse in trial proceedings after collapse of world’s most indebted property developer Business live – latest updates A former steelworker who rose to become one of China’s richest people has pleaded guilty to charges including fundraising fraud after the collapse of Evergrande , the world’s most indebted property developer. The property group’s founder, Hui Ka Yan, “pleaded guilty and expressed remorse” in trial proceedings at a court in China’s southern city of Shenzhen a

stories.title: ‘Nothing but tree skeletons’: record-breaking wildfires devastate US cattle country
stories.summary: Rising temperatures and extreme drought are driving more destructive spring fires across the American Great Plains. This year, forces aligned to create the perfect storm in Nebraska In a normal year, the vast grasslands that roll across the American Great Plains would be starting to green. But at the center of the US, where most of the nation’s beef producers graze their herds, this spring brought fire instead of moisture, leaving more than a million acres black and barren. Multiple blazes raged

stories.title: Anger at ‘bloody unacceptable’ efforts to end Sudan’s war as conflict enters fourth year
stories.summary: A top UN official has criticised lack of global urgency as reports confirm the world’s largest humanitarian crisis is worsening Efforts to end Sudan’s catastrophic war have been criticised as “unacceptable” by the country’s top UN official as a series of new reports confirm that the world’s biggest humanitarian crisis is worsening. Speaking to the Guardian on the eve of the third anniversary of the war, Denise Brown expressed her concern over the apparent lack of political urgency to end a confl

stories.title: Soham murderer Ian Huntley died from ‘blunt head injury’, inquest told
stories.summary: Child killer was allegedly attacked at workshop at HMP Frankland with metal bar and died in hospital An inquest into the death of the Soham murderer, Ian Huntley, has heard he was struck over the head multiple times with a metal bar in prison. Huntley, 52, was an inmate in the maximum-security prison HMP Frankland in Durham, where he was allegedly attacked in a workshop on 26 February. Continue reading...

stories.title: Bahamas police release Michigan man questioned after wife disappeared from their boat
stories.summary: Brian Hooker told police that Lynette Hooker fell overboard and that strong currents carried her away Police in the Bahamas have released without charges a Michigan man who said his wife disappeared after falling overboard from a small boat in waters off the Caribbean island country, authorities said Monday. Brian Hooker, of Onsted in southern Michigan, had been in police custody since 8 April – five days – after being questioned by authorities. Continue reading...

stories.title: IMF warns ‘unprecedented’ energy crisis could trigger global recession as Australia prepares for G20 fuel talks
stories.summary: As treasurer Jim Chalmers weighs ‘extreme uncertainty’, one economic scenario sees global growth plunging to just 2% in 2026 Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The International Monetary Fund has warned the US-Israel war on Iran risks creating an “energy crisis of an unprecedented scale” that could tip the global economy towards recession. The grim warning contained in the IMF’s latest World Economic Outlook comes as Jim Chalmers prepares to attend the organisation’s sp

stories.title: Iran war escalation could trigger global recession, IMF warns
stories.summary: UK suffers sharpest downgrade and is predicted to face joint highest inflation in G7 as growth forecasts are cut Business live – latest updates A further escalation in the Iran war could trigger a global recession, spiralling inflation and a sharp backlash in financial markets, the International Monetary Fund has warned. Against an increasingly volatile backdrop, the Washington-based fund said the economic damage from the Middle East conflict was steadily rising as it cut its growth forecasts fo

stories.title: Trump pick to lead Federal Reserve has assets worth over $100m, disclosures indicate
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh, seeking to replace Fed chair, Jerome Powell, had to file financial disclosures for Senate approval Kevin Warsh, the former Federal Reserve governor chosen by Donald Trump to lead the central bank, has submitted financial disclosures that suggest he holds assets worth well over $100m. The document is required for his nomination to advance through the Senate, beginning with a yet-to-be-scheduled hearing. Continue reading...

stories.title: French cement maker convicted of financing terror groups to keep its Syria plant working
stories.summary: Lafarge fined more than €1m and its former boss jailed for paying nearly €5.6m to groups including Islamic State A French court has fined the cement group Lafarge more than €1m (£870,000) and sentenced its former boss to six years in prison for paying protection money to Islamic State and other terror groups to maintain its business in war-torn Syria from 2013 to 2014. The ruling follows a 2022 case in the United States in which the French firm pleaded guilty to conspiring to provide material su

stories.title: Israeli forces fire teargas at schoolchildren holding West Bank sit-in
stories.summary: Incident took place on first day back at school in small village, as settlers blocked pupils’ access Israeli forces have fired teargas at Palestinian schoolchildren who were staging a sit-in in the occupied West Bank after settlers blocked access to their school. The Israeli military said it had dispersed an “unusual gathering”, but did not specify whether its troops had fired teargas at the children on the first day of class since the start of the Iran war. Continue reading...

The PM is under pressure to implement an Australian-style ban on social media for under-16s, and the government is consulting on whether to go ahead with a full ban, or whether to just impose more specific restrictions. stories.title: Chagos Islands treaty is now ‘impossible to agree at political level’, UK minister says
stories.summary: Stephen Doughty says US withdrawal of support means bill cannot complete passage through parliament A treaty over ceding sovereignty of the Chagos Islands to Mauritius has become “impossible to agree at political level” and the corresponding bill will not complete its passage through parliament, a Foreign Office minister has said. Stephen Doughty told the Commons that the agreement with Mauritius was initially negotiated in close coordination with the US, but Donald Trump’s position “appears to

stories.title: US starts naval blockade of Iranian ports after deadline passes
stories.summary: Iran warns Americans they face higher pump prices due to prohibition imposed on Monday evening Middle East crisis – live updates The US blockade of ships using Iranian ports in the Gulf has come into effect, turning the six-week-old conflict between the US-Israeli coalition and Iran into a test of economic endurance. US Central Command (Centcom) made no formal announcement of the start of the blockade but had said it begin on Monday at 5.30pm Iranian time and would apply to any ships entering or

stories.title: Top US Senate Democrats again try for war powers resolution – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed – our live coverage continues here Circling back to Donald Trump’s coming naval blockade, the US military said it would block all Iranian Gulf ports on Monday at 10am ET on Monday (5.30pm in Iran and 1400 GMT) , effectively seizing control of maritime traffic in the strait of Hormuz. “The blockade will be enforced impartially against vessels of all nations entering or departing Iranian ports and coastal areas, including all Iranian ports on the Arabian Gulf and Gulf of Om

stories.title: Benin's finance minister wins presidential election with 94%
stories.summary: According to provisional results, Romuald Wadagni, the election favorite, won a landslide victory in Benin's presidential election.

stories.title: India news: Ambedkar and the idea of Dalit upliftment
stories.summary: As India marks the 135th birth anniversary of civil rights advocate BR Ambedkar, DW's special coverage explores the gap between the promise of Dalit upliftment and the reality today.

stories.title: China: Evergrande property developer founder pleads guilty
stories.summary: China's property developer giant has become its also its most indebted and defaulted on most of its liabilities and payments in 2021. Founder Hui Ka Yan "pleaded guilty and expressed remorse," the court said.

stories.title: Colombia approves cull of Pablo Escobar-legacy wild hippos
stories.summary: Decades after the drug lord brought in the first hippos for his private zoo, their descendants have become a threat to Colombia's ecosytem.

stories.title: Spanish PM Sanchez' wife charged with corruption
stories.summary: The case is based on a complaint by an anti-corruption group with far-right ties, and is the latest in a string of corruption allegations which have plagued Sanchez' inner circle.

stories.title: South Korea sentences 90‑year‑old woman for money laundering
stories.summary: The woman was helping her son launder drug revenues while he was in prison in Cambodia. The court's one-year prison term took into consideration her age and clean record prior to the laundering.

stories.title: Spanish premier calls for a stronger bond between China and EU on Beijing trip
stories.summary: Spain has been one of Europe's loudest voices calling for expanding trade with China and treating it as a strategic ally, rather than an economic and geopolitical rival.

stories.title: New whale sighting on German-Danish border
stories.summary: A Beluga whale has made its way from the coast of Denmark towards the border with Germany, according to local media. The sighting comes after a humpback whale stranded in the Baltic Sea captivated Germany last month.

stories.title: The German who recovered from a stroke to seal Olympic first
stories.summary: Kathrin Marchand was an elite rower for years before becoming a doctor. But a stroke at the age of 30 changed everything. Despite the challenges, she has now achieved something no other Olympian ever has.

stories.title: Germany's Buchenwald: Remembering Nazi atrocities
stories.summary: Buchenwald was one of the largest concentration camps on German soil. Eighty‑one years ago, it was liberated. The commemoration included moving words — and protests.

stories.title: Bongbong Marcos fights ill-health rumours with star jumps
stories.summary: The Philippine President challenged anyone questioning his fitness to join him in the gym.

stories.title: Trump uses McDonald’s Oval Office stunt to tout new US tip tax break
stories.summary: Donald Trump staged a McDonald’s DoorDash delivery to the Oval Office to promote new tax deductions on tips, touting relief of up to $25,000.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV celebrates historic Mass at rain‑soaked Basilica in Algiers
stories.summary: Algeria hosted Pope Leo XIV on Monday 14 April for a Mass at the Basilica of Our Lady of Africa in Algiers, marking the first papal visit to the country.

stories.title: South Korea jails 90-year-old woman for laundering son's drug money
stories.summary: The woman has been sentenced to a year in jail for laundering money for her son who is imprisoned in Cambodia.

stories.title: Spain seeks closer China links amid global tensions
stories.summary: Pedro Sánchez meets Xi Jinping in Beijing on Tuesday 14 April 2026, aiming to strengthen political and trade relations at a time of global tension.

stories.title: Trump hosts Dutch royals at the White House for dinner and overnight stay
stories.summary: King Willem-Alexander and Queen Máxima of the Netherlands were received by US President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump at the White House on Monday, 13 April 2026, for a formal dinner and overnight stay as part of their three-day visit to the United States.

stories.title: Zelenskyy to meet German Chancellor Merz in Berlin as Russia's war grinds on
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy meets German Chancellor Friedrich Merz in Berlin as Germany emerges as Kyiv’s main backer amid stalled US-led peace talks.

stories.title: Begoña Gómez, wife of Prime Minister Sánchez, to be tried for influence peddling
stories.summary: The decision was announced during the Spanish prime minister's official trip to China, where he is accompanied by his wife.

stories.title: Hormuz standoff turns into a cautious waiting game as US blockade begins
stories.summary: Trump claims Iran made new contact for a deal, as the first hours of the US blockade in the Strait of Hormuz show an apparent cautious approach from all sides as the fragile ceasefire is still holding. But China announces its own shipping transit agreement with Iran, warning the US not to interfere.

stories.title: Sirens halt Israel for Holocaust Remembrance Day as antisemitism surges
stories.summary: Israel observed Holocaust Remembrance Day on Tuesday, 14 April 2026, with a nationwide two-minute silence that brought the country to a halt, as ceremonies at Yad Vashem took place amid growing concern over a global rise in antisemitic incidents and rhetoric.

stories.title: How Harry and Meghan's Australia tour differs to their 2018 royal visit
stories.summary: BBC News journalist Simon Atkinson explains how the couple's tour will be different compared to their last visit.

stories.title: Police officers among seven arrested over deadly Haiti stampede
stories.summary: An investigation is under way into what led to the deaths of 25 people at Laferrière Citadel on Saturday.

stories.title: Spain strengthens ties with China as Sánchez backs Beijing's Middle East role
stories.summary: The Spanish premier announced 19 bilateral agreements with China and urged Beijing to help broker Middle East peace, while avoiding comment on his wife's trial.

stories.title: Gunman opens fire at high school in Turkey, wounding at least 16
stories.summary: The 18-year-old attacker, armed with a shotgun, fired randomly at a high school in Siverek in Şanlıurfa province, before hiding inside the building.

stories.title: Can AI systems replace human judges and lawyers?
stories.summary: Can AI make fundamentally ethical decisions? If it delivers an unlawful verdict, who will be punished? In the pursuit of efficiency and speed, can we trust people's fates to artificial intelligence?

stories.title: Algeria hit by two suicide attacks during Pope Leo XIV's visit, sources say
stories.summary: No link has yet been established between the attacks and the pope's visit to the country, which was conducted under tight security.

stories.title: French woman, 86, held by ICE after moving to US to reunite with long-lost love
stories.summary: The son of pensioner Marie-Thérèse told French media he is worried for his mother's frail health as she is detained in a Louisiana ICE centre.

stories.title: Cameroon separatists to pause fighting ahead of Pope visit
stories.summary: The separatists say their decison reflects responsibility, restraint and respect for human dignity.

stories.title: Sixteen injured after ex-student opens fire at high school in Turkey
stories.summary: Students and teachers are among those who were injured in the attack in the country's south-east.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV says he has 'no fear' of Trump administration
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV said he has no intention to debate with Donald Trump after the US president criticised him over his comments on the Iran war. "I have no fear of the Trump administration nor of speaking out loudly about the message of the gospel," the pope told reporters aboard the papal plane.

stories.title: Ex-Nigerian oil minister denies taking bribes
stories.summary: Diezani Alison-Madueke is accused of living “a life of luxury” in the UK at properties paid for and refurbished by industry figures.

stories.summary: Such misfires are common in Nigeria, where the military often conducts air raids to battle armed groups who control vast forest enclaves.

stories.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

stories.title: Nigeria Air Force orders investigation after dozens killed in airstrike
stories.summary: There are reports that more than 100 people were killed, although the authorities have not commented.

stories.title: Pope Leo visits Algeria in sign of Africa’s growing importance to Catholic church
stories.summary: Pontiff makes first papal visit to country as he starts 11-day tour that will also include stops in Cameroon and Angola Pope Leo XIV has arrived in Algeria for the first papal visit to the country, calling for peace on the opening stop of a tour of Africa that signals the continent’s growing importance to the Catholic church. The 11-day trip, which will include stops in Cameroon, Angola and Equatorial Guinea, is the longest by Pope Leo since being elected to the papacy in May last year. Continue

The environment minister, Irene Vélez, said the decision was reached because other methods to control their population had been expensive and unsuccessful, includ

stories.title: Bahamas police again question US man over disappearance of wife at sea
stories.summary: Brian Hooker says wife Lynette fell overboard from dinghy but family members have cast doubt on that account Police in the Bahamas on Monday were set to again interview a US man who said his wife fell overboard from their boat. In a statement on Sunday to the Guardian, Brian Hooker’s attorney, Terrel Butler, said: “The police have requested another interview with [Brian Hooker] tomorrow.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump's naval blockade raises economic pressure on Iran
stories.summary: A US naval blockade will cut off Iran's main source of hard currency by halting most of its oil exports. Will Donald Trump's pressure force Tehran back to negotiations or widen the conflict?

stories.title: Lufthansa pilots launch 2-day strike, cabin crew say they will strike right after
stories.summary: Pilots who are members of the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union are striking on Monday and Tuesday. As hundreds of flights were canceled, the UFO cabin crew union said it would also strike on Wednesday and Thursday.

stories.title: US judge dismisses Trump's lawsuit against Wall Street Journal
stories.summary: The judge threw out Donald Trump's lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal over a story on the US president's ties to late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

stories.title: US: Fugitive Brazilian ex-spy chief arrested by ICE
stories.summary: Former Brazilian intelligence chief Alexandre Ramagem has been detained by ICE in the United States. Brazil formally requested his extradition in December.

stories.title: US blockade of Iranian ports comes into effect as Trump threatens to destroy 'attack ships'
stories.summary: In a social media post on Sunday, Trump said his goal was to clear the Strait of Hormuz of mines and reopen it to all shipping, but that Iran must not be allowed to profit from controlling the key cargo waterway.

stories.title: The US blockade of Iran is a gamble. Will it work?
stories.summary: The US military is gambling on the blockade denying Iran vital trade - but questions remain on whether the bet will pay off.

stories.title: Iran executed at least 1,639 people in 2025 in 68% increase on year before, NGOs say
stories.summary: Rights groups including Amnesty International say Iran carries out the most executions of any nation worldwide per capita, and the most of any country other than China, for which no reliable data is available.

stories.title: Israel summons Italy's ambassador over comments on 'unacceptable attacks' on Lebanon
stories.summary: Lebanon was pulled into the Middle East war when Iran-backed group Hezbollah fired rockets at Israel on 2 March, days after the opening salvo of US-Israeli strikes killed Iran's supreme leader Ali Khamenei.

stories.title: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism
stories.summary: Italy's prime minister and the US president are close allies, but Trump has refused to apologise to the "very weak" Pope Leo XIV.

stories.title: US drivers head to Native American lands for cheaper gas

stories.title: Germany marks 69th birthday of zoo’s oldest gorilla
stories.summary: Berlin Zoo celebrates Fatou, a 69‑year‑old western lowland gorilla, highlighting how specialised care in captivity can extend the lives of endangered species.

stories.title: Philippines accuses Chinese fishermen of dumping cyanide in South China Sea

stories.title: Hezbollah official says group will not abide by any agreements from Lebanon-Israel talks
stories.summary: Israel and Hezbollah have fought multiple wars since the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group was formed in the 1980s as a guerrilla force fighting against Israel’s occupation of southern Lebanon at the time.

stories.title: Spanish PM's wife charged with corruption after two-year probe
stories.summary: Begoña Gómez is accused of using her relationship with Pedro Sánchez to advance her private career.

stories.title: 'They are striking everywhere' - Paramedics in Lebanon speak to BBC after Israeli strikes
stories.summary: BBC Middle East correspondent Hugo Bachega joins paramedics on duty in Nabatieh - a city that was once vibrant, but is now abandoned.

stories.title: Eric Swalwell to resign from Congress after sexual misconduct claims
stories.summary: Four women have accused the congressman and ex-candidate for California governor of misconduct ranging from sexual harassment to rape, which he has denied.

stories.title: Democrats were already scrambling in California's governor race. Then Swalwell dropped out
stories.summary: California Democrats were already fretting about the race due to troubling polls and low name recognition

stories.summary: The Lebanese government go into peace talks with limited influence over the group.

stories.title: Hospital at centre of child HIV outbreak caught reusing syringes in undercover filming

stories.title: Can this man broaden the appeal of a South African party seen by some as 'too white'?
stories.summary: The Democratic Alliance's new leader, Geordin Hill-Lewis, admits the party's support is too narrow.

stories.summary: With some promising computing companies in the field, could Europe be a leader in quantum tech?

stories.title: A Raiffeisen vezető elemzője szerint a költségvetés nagy kihívás lesz az új kormánynak, el kell kezdeni keményen dolgozni

stories.title: Az USA szerint most Iránon a sor, hogy lépjen
stories.summary: Irán felajánlotta, hogy öt évre hajlandó felfüggeszteni nukleáris tevékenységét, de Amerika ragaszkodik a húsz évhez.

stories.summary: A Fitch Ratings szerint viszont a kormányváltás után nagyobb az esély, hogy a befagyasztott EU-s pénzek megérkeznek, és arra sem számítanak, hogy az állami intézmények keresztbe tesznek majd az új kormánynak.

stories.title: Trump szerint ő nem Jézus, csak egy orvos a képen, amin kézrátétellel és fénnyel gyógyít
stories.summary: „Orvosként nekem kellene jobbá tennem az embereket, és én jobbá is teszem őket” – mondta Trump.

stories.summary: A politológus szerint a Tisza közel 1 millió győzteskompenzációs szavazatra számíthat, ami 5-6 listás mandátumot jelenthet.

stories.title: Még a héten folytatódhatnak az iráni–amerikai tárgyalások Pakisztánban
stories.summary: A hétvégén eredmények nélkül zárultak a megbeszélések.

stories.title: Mészáros Lőrinc cégcsoportja a választás után is biztos „pénzügyi háttérrel” és „határozott stratégiai vízióval” rendelkezik
stories.summary: A Mészáros-csoport honlapja a választás utáni napon elérhetetlenné vált, majd frissült a weboldal, hivatalosan karbantartás folyik az oldalon.

stories.title: Szolnok behúzta a féket, nem fizetik be a szolidaritási hozzájárulást az új kormány megalakulásáig
stories.summary: Az önkormányzatoknak április közepéig kellene befizetniük a szolidaritási hozzájárulás második részletét.

stories.title: Szeifert Natália kapta a Békés Pál-díjat
stories.summary: Az elismerésről a Békés Pál Civil Társaság szakmai kuratóriuma döntött.

stories.title: Bocsánatot kért a dolgozóitól a választás előtti leveléért a Bayer Construct vezetője
stories.summary: „Nem volt szándékomban megsérteni senkinek a szabad döntését és választási akaratát” – írja Balázs Attila.

stories.title: Öntsünk tiszta vizet a pohárba – segít egy jó vízadagoló!
stories.summary: Vannak a munkahelyi létnek olyan elemei – a jó kávé vagy éppen a minőségi víz – melyek meglepően sokat tesznek a jóllétünkhöz, így a hatékonyságunkhoz is. A szűrt víz finomabb és egészségesebb, mint a csapvíz, és a környezetet is kevésbé terheli, mintha palackos vizet fogyasztanánk, tudtuk meg a Telex számára is vizet kínáló aboutwater ügyvezetőjétől, Krégl Jánostól.

stories.title: A magány rontja a memóriát, de nem gyorsítja fel a leépülést egy friss kutatás szerint
stories.summary: Több mint 10 ezer embert éveken át vizsgáltak.

stories.title: Lövöldözött egy volt diák egy törökországi iskolában, tizenhatan megsérültek
stories.summary: A támadó végzett magával.

stories.title: Bűnösnek vallotta magát a becsődölt kínai ingatlanóriás vezetője
stories.summary: Az Evergrande csődje válságba taszította a kínai ingatlanpiacot, a cég vezetője közben offshore számlákra mentette a cég vagyonát.

stories.title: Mindenki vegye fel a telefont, ha csörög, végezzétek a munkát, üzente az MCC-seknek a főigazgatójuk
stories.summary: Szalai Zoltán főigazgató kedden 11 órára hirdetett állománygyűlést, amin arra kérte a dolgozókat, hogy álljanak ki az intézményt ért támadások ellen.

stories.title: Politikusként eddig lehetett úgy eljutni, hogy az ember nem hazudik, meg nem lop – interjú Hadházy Ákossal
stories.summary: Hogyan élte meg a vereséget, és miért döntött úgy, hogy visszalép a politikából? Hogyan tekint vissza politikai karrierjére? Élő interjúztunk Hadházy Ákos leköszönő parlamenti képviselővel.

stories.title: Volt jövőbe látó MI, ami jól megtippelte a választási eredményt?
stories.summary: A Super Bowl teljesen kifogott a nagy csetbotokon, az Oscar csak kettőt gáncsolt el. Nézzük, mennyire pontosan tippelték meg a választási eredményt!

stories.title: Karácsony Gergely: Ha az Államkincstár inkasszálja a főváros számláját, az áprilisi béreket sem fogjuk tudni kifizetni
stories.summary: A főpolgármester hétfőn levelet írt az Államkincstár vezetőjének, amiben arra kérte, áprilisban ne inkasszálja újra a Fővárosi Önkormányzat folyószámláját.

stories.title: Az iráni háború miatt akadozik az üzemanyag-ellátás, a légitársaságok az EU beavatkozását sürgetik
stories.summary: Európában három héten belül rendszerszintű kerozinhiány léphet fel.

stories.title: Bencsik Gábor Bayer Zsoltot okolta a Fidesz vereségéért a Facebookon, majd nem nyilvánosra állította a posztját
stories.summary: Bayerről azt írta, az induláskor „erős irodalmi, kivételes publicisztikai tehetség […], a legjobb haver”, ami később átcsúszott „a mindenkit kiosztásba, a mások leugatásába”.

stories.title: Esterházy Péter születésnapján két friss könyvvel idézhetjük fel, hogy neki a művészet volt a világnézete
stories.summary: A friss interjúkötetben az író fociról, gasztronómiáról, gyereknevelésről és közügyekről beszél, az esszéiben pedig a nyelv csodáiról.

stories.title: Azbeszttel szennyezett aszfalttal borították le Szombathely egyik városrészét, büntetőeljárás indult
stories.summary: A kőzetanyagot osztrák bányákból szállították a városba, az Oladi Plató nevű városrész összes utcája érintett lehet.

stories.title: Katy Perry sajtósa szerint totális kamu, hogy az énekesnő húsz éve szexuálisan zaklatott egy ausztrál színésznőt
stories.summary: Ruby Rose nemrég arról írt, egy melbourne-i szórakozóhelyen Perry az arcához dörgölte a nemi szervét, amikor még csak 20 éves volt.

stories.title: Hamar új korszakra ébredt a Petőfi TV a választások után
stories.summary: A Petőfi TV műsorvezetője április 13-án azt mondta, szeretnének nyitni azok felé az alkotók felé is, akik eddig még nem szerepeltek náluk.

stories.title: Republikon: A Tisza az országjárással a legkisebb településeken is átvette a vezetést
stories.summary: Csak az 500 lakos alatti kistelepüléseken maradt meg a Fidesz markáns előnye.

stories.title: Anthony Albanese urges Israel to stop Lebanon attacks that intensified during Middle East ceasefire
stories.summary: PM tells Guardian Australia Hezbollah should cease reprisals and confirms Australia’s military surveillance aircraft will remain in region Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s prime minister, Anthony Albanese, has urged Israel to stop its attacks on Lebanon and raised concern over its intensified military campaign on Beirut and the country’s south after the ceasefire in the Middle East. Albanese also calle

stories.title: Merck's Keytruda: A lifesaving drug, a global divide
stories.summary: A cross-border investigation by DW and ICIJ reveals how pricing and patents helped turn a life-saving medicine into one of the world's best selling drugs while leaving many patients struggling to access it.

stories.title: Peru faces protests as election delays fuel doubts over vote integrity
stories.summary: Peru’s presidential election on Sunday, 12 April 2026, was thrown into uncertainty after widespread logistical failures prevented thousands of voters from casting their ballots, delaying the outcome.

stories.title: Trump lambasts Pope Leo XIV in ongoing feud with Catholic Church leader over Iran war
stories.summary: The US president launches an unprecedented attack on Pope Leo XIV over the pontiff's rejection of the Iran war, accusing him of being "weak on crime" and hurting the Catholic Church.

stories.title: Germany cuts fuel tax as oil prices surge on Iran blockade
stories.summary: Following tense exchanges between Economics Minister Reiche and Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil, the government held talks over the weekend. Will the coalition succeed in easing the burden on citizens, or will it collapse?

stories.title: Union Berlin pushes back on sexist comments aimed at trailblazing coach Marie-Louise Eta
stories.summary: Eta has been named interim coach for the last five games of the season as Union aims to avoid relegation from Germany's Bundesliga.

stories.title: Iran carried out highest number of executions in decades
stories.summary: Organisations warn many more people could be executed in the wake of the anti-government protests which swept Iran earlier this year.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Super Typhoon Sinlaku threatens Mariana Islands
stories.summary: Rapidly strengthening storm brings destructive winds, flooding risk and dangerous seas to western Pacific Dangerous super typhoon barreling toward group of remote US islands The Mariana Islands archipelago in the western Pacific, home to the US territories of Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands, are bracing for extreme weather early this week as Super Typhoon Sinlaku approaches. The system originated as a cluster of thunderstorms over the seas of Micronesia before strengthening into a tropical

stories.title: What is the new EU bill and could it give UK ministers Henry VIII-type powers?
stories.summary: Legislation would allow government to implement evolving single market rules without full parliamentary scrutiny Ministers in Britain are planning a new bill that would bring into force a food and drink trade deal with the EU but also contain powers enabling the government to “dynamically align” with Europe. It would allow the UK to quickly implement evolving single market rules if it determines it is in the national interest, without having to face full parliamentary scrutiny. Continue reading.
